text_original: Tong Ren (or 'Union of men') appears here (as we find it) in the (remote districts of the) country, indicating progress and success. It will be advantageous to cross the great stream. It will be advantageous to maintain the firm correctness of the superior man.
text_normalized: Tong Ren (or 'Union of men') appears here (as we find it) in the (remote districts of the) country, indicating progress and success. It will be advantageous to cross the great stream. It will be advantageous to maintain the firm correctness of the superior man.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=Judgment.

text_original: The first NINE, undivided, (shows the representative of) the union of men just issuing from his gate. There will be no error.
text_normalized: The first NINE, undivided, (shows the representative of) the union of men just issuing from his gate. There will be no error.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=first.

text_original: The second SIX, divided, (shows the representative of) the union of men in relation with his kindred. There will be occasion for regret.
text_normalized: The second SIX, divided, (shows the representative of) the union of men in relation with his kindred. There will be occasion for regret.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=second.

text_original: The third NINE, undivided, (shows its subject) with his arms hidden in the thick grass, and at the top of a high mound. (But) for three years he makes no demonstration.
text_normalized: The third NINE, undivided, (shows its subject) with his arms hidden in the thick grass, and at the top of a high mound. (But) for three years he makes no demonstration.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=third.

text_original: The fourth NINE, undivided, (shows its subject) mounted on the city wall; but he does not proceed to make the attack (he contemplates). There will be good fortune.
text_normalized: The fourth NINE, undivided, (shows its subject) mounted on the city wall; but he does not proceed to make the attack (he contemplates). There will be good fortune.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fourth.

text_original: In the fifth NINE, undivided, (the representative of) the union of men first wails and cries out, and then laughs. His great host conquers, and he (and the subject of the second line) meet together.
text_normalized: In the fifth NINE, undivided, (the representative of) the union of men first wails and cries out, and then laughs. His great host conquers, and he (and the subject of the second line) meet together.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=fifth.

text_original: The topmost NINE, undivided, (shows the representative of) the union of men in the suburbs. There will be no occasion for repentance.
text_normalized: The topmost NINE, undivided, (shows the representative of) the union of men in the suburbs. There will be no occasion for repentance.
notes: James Legge translation parsed from ChineseNotes bilingual Yijing file; role=top.
